summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Mass-spring system'?

"Mass-spring system" means: A mass m on a spring with constant k oscillates with ω = √(k/m) and T = 2π√(m/k). The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Wave equation'?

"Wave equation" means: y(x,t) = A sin(kx - ωt); describes a sinusoidal traveling wave with wave number k = 2π/λ and angular frequency ω = 2πf. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Wave speed'?

"Wave speed" means: v = λf = ω/k; the speed at which the wave pattern propagates through the medium. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Transverse vs. longitudinal'?

"Transverse vs. longitudinal" means: Transverse: displacement perpendicular to wave direction (e.g., string); Longitudinal: displacement parallel (e.g., sound). The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Superposition'?

"Superposition" means: When two waves overlap, the resultant displacement is the sum of individual displacements; leads to interference and standing waves. The other options describe different concepts.

summary_sections.title: Special Topics
summary_sections.content_html:
Important Concepts

Which term is described by: "y(x,t) = A sin(kx - ωt); describes a sinusoidal traveling wave with wave number k = 2π/λ and angular frequency ω = 2πf"

This description refers to "Wave equation".
